verses.text: An altar of shittim wood, five by five by three cubits, foursquare. Horns at four corners, of one piece; overlay with brass. All vessels — pans, shovels, basins, fleshhooks, firepans — brass. A brass grate of network, four rings, under the altar's compass. Shittim staves overlaid with brass bear it. Hollow boards, after the pattern. Guidance: The altar stands first — bigger than the ark. The way to God's house begins at fire and blood. Every other piece rests on what is paid here.

The court: south side fine linen hangings one hundred cubits, twenty pillars, brass sockets, silver hooks. Same north. West fifty cubits, ten pillars. East fifty — fifteen-cubit hangings each side, three pillars; gate twenty cubits of blue, purple, scarlet needlework, four pillars. Silver hooks, brass sockets and pins. One hundred long, fifty wide, five high. Guidance: A linen wall, sixty pillars — one gate, twenty cubits wide, embroidered in priestly colors. God's house is enclosed, but the door is generous.

Command Israel to bring pure beaten olive oil to keep the lamp burning always. In the tabernacle outside the vail before the testimony, Aaron and his sons shall order it from evening to morning before the LORD — a statute forever. Guidance: The lamp must never go out. Israel keeps providing oil; the priests keep tending the flame. God's light is not magic — it is daily faithfulness refusing to let it go dark.
